extraction.one_line_summary: ALAPHILIPPE Julian won after overtaking VAN AVERMAET on the final Mur de Huy ascent, breaking VALVERDE's four-year winning streak
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step placed multiple riders including TOPSKAERT and DECLERCQ in early break, pre-positioning for Mur finale control
VAN AVERMAET attacked solo entering the valley before Huy, gaining 4-15 seconds but burned matches before final climb
ALAPHILIPPE waited until final Mur ascent to accelerate despite having teammates forward, refused to chase earlier

rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: third podium in three participations before breakthrough win, suggesting progressive climb mastery on Mur de Huy
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: negative: five-time winner beaten to second after four consecutive victories, showing age vulnerability on steep finale
rider_signals.VAN AVERMAET Greg: negative: Milan-San Remo winner's solo attack caught on final climb, classics power insufficient for Mur gradient
rider_signals.GILBERT Philippe: neutral: fourth place suggests BMC lacked numerical advantage compared to Quick-Step's multi-rider positioning
